tweetText: IBM is launching Bob, an AI platform aimed at managing software delivery costs and governance. This is important as it helps companies balance rapid coding with the need for compliance and control over technical debt. via AI News

AI is evolving from basic models to advanced multimodal systems, highlighting the crucial role of encoders in understanding and processing information. This shift matters because it enhances AI's ability to interpret complex data effectively. via AI News.

Kakao Mobility has unveiled its plans for developing Level 4 autonomous driving technologies, presented at the 2026 World IT Show. This move is significant as it underscores the company's commitment to advancing physical AI and autonomous services. via AI News

Lightelligence's stock soared 400% on debut, briefly reaching a $10 billion market cap despite only $15.5 million in revenue. This jump highlights investor confidence in optical interconnects as a key solution for AI's future challenges. via AI News.

Musk revisited his past friendship with OpenAI during his trial, sharing details he had previously disclosed in interviews and his biography. This highlights the ongoing impact of personal relationships in the tech industry. via TechCrunch AI

Amazon has launched new OpenAI products on AWS, including a new agent service, just after Microsoft dropped its exclusivity agreement. This move enhances competition in the AI space and gives more options for developers. via TechCrunch AI

Amazon just launched a feature called "Join the chat" that lets you ask product questions and get AI audio answers. This makes shopping more interactive and helps customers get information quickly. via TechCrunch AI

Google has signed a new contract to provide the Pentagon with access to its AI, following Anthropic's refusal to participate. This move raises concerns about the use of AI in military surveillance and weaponry. via TechCrunch AI.

YouTube is testing a new AI search feature that provides guided answers for Premium subscribers in the U.S. This could change how users find content and enhance their viewing experience. via TechCrunch AI
Neurable is licensing its non-invasive "mind-reading" tech for consumer wearables. This could lead to innovative new products that enhance how we interact with technology. via TechCrunch AI
